parameters.query: SELECT c.id AS category_id, c.nom AS sous_categorie, parent.nom AS categorie_parent FROM public.categories c LEFT JOIN public.categories parent ON c.parent_id = parent.id WHERE c.slug = $1 LIMIT 1;

nodes.name: Get category from slug
nodes.notes: Credential à créer : Postgres pointant vers db.qfdvdcvqknoqfxetttch.supabase.co (port 5432, user postgres, mdp = celui fourni à la création du projet, db postgres, SSL)

parameters.query: INSERT INTO public.reports (category_id, sous_categorie, top_n, status, slug_public, owner_email) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, 'running', $4, $5) RETURNING id, sous_categorie, top_n;

parameters.text: =Tu es un analyste sectoriel B2B. Identifie les {{ $json.top_n }} sociétés mondiales les plus importantes du secteur "{{ $json.sous_categorie }}" en {{ $json.year }}. Pour chaque société : nom officiel, domaine principal (ex: blackrock.com), pays, ville du siège, fourchette d'effectifs, description en 1 phrase, métrique clé du secteur (nom/valeur/unité/année), 3 sources web vérifiées (URL, titre, éditeur). Réponse en JSON STRICT, sans préambule, sans backticks. Schéma : {"metadata":{"category":"...","year":...,"top_n_requested":...,"language":"fr"},"companies":[{"rank":1,"name":"...","domain":"...","country":"...","city":"...","employees_range":"...","description":"...","key_metric":{"name":"...","value":"...","unit":"...","as_of_year":2025},"sources":[{"url":"https://...","title":"...","publisher":"..."}]}]}

=Quelles sont les {{ $json.top_n }} sociétés majeures du secteur "{{ $json.sous_categorie }}" dans le monde en {{ $json.year }} ? Utilise web search pour des données fraîches. Pour chaque société : nom, domaine, pays, ville, effectifs, description (1 phrase), métrique clé (nom/valeur/unité/année), 3 sources (URL/titre/éditeur), confidence (high/medium/low). Réponse JSON STRICT, sans backticks. Schéma : {"metadata":{"category":"...","year":...,"top_n_requested":...,"data_freshness_note":"...","language":"fr"},"companies":[{"rank":1,"name":"...","domain":"...","country":"...","city":"...","employees_range":"...","description":"...","key_metric":{"name":"...","value":"...","unit":"...","as_of_year":2025},"sources":[],"confidence":"high|medium|low"}]}

=Question d'étude sur la perception sectorielle par les LLM. Quelles sont les {{ $json.top_n }} sociétés majeures du secteur "{{ $json.sous_categorie }}" dans le monde, basé sur tes données d'entraînement uniquement (cutoff fin 2024) ? Sois honnête : moins de sociétés bien connues vaut mieux qu'une longue liste hallucinée. JSON STRICT, sans backticks. Schéma : {"metadata":{"category":"...","year":...,"top_n_requested":...,"training_cutoff_note":"...","language":"fr"},"companies":[{"rank":1,"name":"...","domain":"...","country":"...","city":"...","employees_range":"...","description":"...","key_metric":{"name":"...","value":"...","unit":"...","as_of_year":2024},"sources":[],"confidence":"high|medium|low"}]}

=Étude de perception sectorielle inter-LLM : quelles sont les {{ $json.top_n }} sociétés majeures du secteur "{{ $json.sous_categorie }}" dans le monde, basé sur tes données d'entraînement (cutoff début 2025) ? Honnêteté > complétude. JSON STRICT, sans backticks. Schéma : {"metadata":{"category":"...","year":...,"top_n_requested":...,"training_cutoff_note":"...","language":"fr"},"companies":[{"rank":1,"name":"...","domain":"...","country":"...","city":"...","employees_range":"...","description":"...","key_metric":{"name":"...","value":"...","unit":"...","as_of_year":2024},"sources":[],"confidence":"high|medium|low"}]}

parameters.query: INSERT INTO public.raw_responses (report_id, provider, model, prompt, response_json, sources_json) SELECT (($1::jsonb)->>'report_id')::uuid, r->>'provider', r->>'model', r->>'prompt', (r->'response_json'), (r->'sources_json') FROM jsonb_array_elements(($1::jsonb)->'raw_responses') AS r;

WITH input_companies AS (SELECT (c->>'name') AS nom, (c->>'name_normalized') AS nom_normalise, NULLIF(c->>'domain','') AS domain, NULLIF(c->>'country','') AS country, NULLIF(c->>'employees_range','') AS employees_range, NULLIF(c->>'best_description','') AS description, (c->>'rank_consolidated')::int AS rank, (c->>'visibility_score')::int AS visibility_score, NULLIF(c->>'avg_position','null')::numeric AS avg_position, (c->>'source_count')::int AS source_count, (c->'cited_by') AS cited_by FROM jsonb_array_elements(($1::jsonb)->'companies') AS c), upsert_companies AS (INSERT INTO public.companies (nom, nom_normalise, domain, country, employees_range, description) SELECT nom, nom_normalise, domain, country, employees_range, description FROM input_companies ON CONFLICT (nom_normalise, domain) DO UPDATE SET last_enriched_at = NOW() RETURNING id, nom_normalise, domain) INSERT INTO public.report_companies (report_id, company_id, rank, cited_by, visibility_score, avg_position_in_lists, source_count) SELECT (($1::jsonb)->>'report_id')::uuid, uc.id, ic.rank, ic.cited_by, ic.visibility_score, ic.avg_position, ic.source_count FROM input_companies ic JOIN upsert_companies uc ON uc.nom_normalise = ic.nom_normalise AND COALESCE(uc.domain,'') = COALESCE(ic.domain,'') ON CONFLICT (report_id, company_id) DO NOTHING;
UPDATE public.reports SET status = 'ready', completed_at = NOW() WHERE id = $1::uuid RETURNING id, status;

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { ok: true, report_id: $('Consolidate (JS)').item.json.report_id, sous_categorie: $('Consolidate (JS)').item.json.sous_categorie, stats: $('Consolidate (JS)').item.json.consolidated.stats, top_5: $('Consolidate (JS)').item.json.consolidated.companies.slice(0,5).map(c => ({ rank: c.rank_consolidated, name: c.name, visibility: c.visibility_score + '/4' })) } }}
